title: Eschenmoser Methenylation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Eschenmoser methenylation describing installation of an exocyclic methylene equivalent at the alpha-position of carbonyl compounds by reaction of enolates with Eschenmoser’s salt followed by elimination from the resulting aminomethylated intermediate.
notes: Key concepts: enolate trapping with Eschenmoser’s salt, formation of alpha-(dimethylamino)methyl carbonyl intermediates, oxidative or Hofmann/Cope-type elimination, and access to alpha-methylenated ketones or lactones.

title: Eschenmoser Methenylation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for Eschenmoser methenylation featuring exo-methylene hydroazulenone formation, one-pot access to alpha-exo-methylene lactones in the CP-263,114 series, alpha-methylene installation in crassin acetate methyl ether, and bicyclic oxetane preparation en route to (+)-gelsemine.
notes: Examples emphasize LiHMDS/LiTMP or LDA-mediated enolate formation, use of Eschenmoser’s salt, oxidative N-oxide formation or exhaustive methylation/elimination, and deployment of the resulting exo-methylene group in natural-product synthesis.

title: Eschenmoser-Claisen Rearrangement – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Claisen rearrangement describing Claisen-type [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of allylic or benzylic alcohols heated with N,N-dimethylacetamide dimethyl acetal to furnish gamma,delta-unsaturated amides through ketene N,O-acetal intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: allylic alcohol exchange with DMA-DMA, ketene aminal / ketene N,O-acetal formation, chairlike or boatlike transition-state control, and stereoselective generation of gamma,delta-unsaturated amides.

title: Eschenmoser-Claisen Rearrangement – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Eschenmoser-Claisen rearrangement featuring syntheses of (+)-stenine, (+)-pravastatin, (+)-8-deoxyanisatin, and the AB ring system of norzoanthamine through stereocontrolled allylic-amide rearrangement chemistry.
notes: Examples emphasize high-temperature rearrangement with DMA-DMA, installation of quaternary stereocenters, and use of rearranged amides as versatile synthetic handles in complex natural-product synthesis.

title: Eschenmoser-Tanabe Fragmentation – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Eschenmoser-Tanabe fragmentation describing conversion of tosylhydrazones of alpha,beta-epoxy ketones into cyclic alkynones, alkynals, or acyclic alkynones by base-, acid-, or heat-induced fragmentation with loss of nitrogen.
notes: Key concepts: epoxyketone hydrazone formation, Grob-type fragmentation, release of N2 and sulfinate leaving groups, and formation of medium-sized cyclic or acyclic alkynes / alkynones from epoxy ketone precursors.

title: Eschenmoser-Tanabe Fragmentation – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Eschenmoser-Tanabe fragmentation featuring preparation of the Galbulimima alkaloid GB 13 alkynone, alkyl-substituted enol lactones from alkynoid ketones, unstable alkynals converted to 1,6-enynes, and cycloalkynone generation for Dötz benzannulation-based aromatic annulation studies.
notes: Examples emphasize epoxidation–hydrazone formation–fragmentation sequences, rapid trapping of reactive alkynals, and use of the resulting alkynones or alkynals in downstream metathesis or benzannulation chemistry.

title: Eschweiler-Clarke Methylation (Reductive Alkylation) – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for Eschweiler-Clarke methylation describing one-pot reductive methylation of primary or secondary amines with formaldehyde and formic acid, together with related reductive alkylation and cyclization variants using carbonyl compounds and reducing agents.
notes: Key concepts: iminium formation from amine and formaldehyde, hydride transfer from formic acid, selective N-methylation without overalkylation to quaternary salts, and relationship to Leuckart-Wallach-type reductive alkylation.

title: Eschweiler-Clarke Methylation (Reductive Alkylation) – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for Eschweiler-Clarke methylation featuring modified N,N-dimethylamine installation in a calyculin A/B derivative, one-pot formation of (S)-N-methylanabasine, mild formaldehyde/cyanoborohydride methylation en route to (-)-horsfiline, and macrocyclic triazamethylation under Eschweiler-Clarke conditions.
notes: Examples emphasize classical HCHO/HCO2H conditions as well as milder reductive alkylation variants using NaBH3CN or deprotection-hydrogenation sequences followed by formaldehyde-mediated methylation.

title: Evans Aldol Reaction – Importance and Mechanism
summary: Overview page for the Evans aldol reaction describing boron-mediated, chiral auxiliary-controlled aldol addition of N-acyl oxazolidinones to aldehydes through Z-boron enolates, delivering syn aldol adducts with high diastereoselectivity via Zimmerman-Traxler transition states.
notes: Key concepts: Bu2BOTf-mediated enolization of oxazolidinones, Z-enolate control, Zimmerman-Traxler transition-state model, and syn-selective carbon–carbon bond formation using Evans auxiliaries.

title: Evans Aldol Reaction – Synthetic Applications
summary: Application page for the Evans aldol reaction featuring syntheses of glucolipsin A, the pentapeptide S fragment of bleomycin A2, and (-)-FR182877 through highly syn-selective boron-mediated aldol additions of chiral oxazolidinone derivatives to complex aldehydes.
notes: Examples emphasize excellent syn diastereoselectivity, use of Bu2BOTf/DIPEA or boron enolate conditions, and direct incorporation of aldol fragments into advanced natural-product synthesis campaigns.
